summary: Obfuscated bytenode module loading opaque Windows-x64 native addon
details: Package main entry loads `./index.jsc`, a bytenode-compiled V8 bytecode module whose decompiled source is processed by js-confuser: all string constants (URLs, function names, paths) are reconstructed at runtime from per-character code tables, preventing static audit. The obfuscated module dlopens a sibling 676KB Windows-x64-only native addon at `build/Release/debugelevator.node` (name suggestive of 'debug elevator' / privilege elevation) and invokes methods derived from the obfuscated string tables. No source for the native addon is shipped. Package metadata is placeholder-quality (homepage/repository/bugs all point at `github.com/yourusername/baksen`, description is the literal word `baksen`, no README), and the decoded bytecode contains partially scrubbed `@redacted/...` references suggesting a skeleton lifted from a third-party tool. The harmful surface (if any) is reachable only when a consumer requires the module or invokes the CLI — there is no install-time lifecycle script and the obfuscated code does not auto-execute on `npm install`. The combination of bytenode obfuscation, opaque native binary, placeholder publisher identity, and suggestive addon naming prevents any benign explanation from being verified, but no concrete exfiltration, C2 endpoint, credential read, or dropper behavior has been observed.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
